note_data.title: Introduction to accounting
keyPoints: **Accounting** is the recording, classifying and summarizing of financial transactions and communicating the results to interested parties
The main purpose of accounting is to **provide financial information** to help users make informed economic decisions
**Internal users** include managers, owners and employees who need information for planning, controlling and decision-making
**External users** include investors, lenders, suppliers, customers and government who need information to assess financial performance
Accounting provides information about **financial position** (what the business owns and owes) and **financial performance** (profit or loss)
**Stewardship** means managers are accountable to owners for how they have used the business resources
Accounting helps in **planning** future activities, **controlling** current operations and **decision-making** for the business
Financial information must be **relevant, reliable, comparable and understandable** to be useful to users

definitions.definition: The recording, classifying and summarizing of financial transactions and communicating the results to interested parties
People within the business who use accounting information for planning, controlling and decision-making
People outside the business who use accounting information to make economic decisions about the business
The responsibility of managers to account to owners for how they have used the business resources
What the business owns (assets) and what it owes (liabilities) at a particular point in time

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Name or select the correct answer from information given
Give the main features or characteristics without explanation

modelAnswer.question: Explain two purposes of accounting. [4 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: One purpose is to provide financial information to users because this helps them make informed economic decisions about the business. Another purpose is stewardship because managers need to account to owners for how they have used the business resources.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Provide financial information to users
Mark 2: To help make informed economic decisions
Mark 4: Managers account to owners for use of resources

commonMistakes: Never say accounting is 'just bookkeeping' — always say it includes communicating results to users
Students often confuse internal and external users — remember managers are internal, investors are external
Don't say accounting 'makes profit' — say it 'measures and reports profit'
Never give examples as definitions — define the term then give examples separately
Students forget that stewardship means accountability to owners, not just managing resources
examinerTips: This topic appears every year as foundation knowledge for other topics
Always distinguish between recording transactions and communicating results
Questions often ask for users and their information needs — learn specific examples
Purpose questions need the reason WHY accounting is done, not just WHAT it does
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Accounting process flow: Financial transactions → Recording → Classifying → Summarizing → Communicating to internal users (managers, owners, employees) and external users (investors, lenders, suppliers, government)
